summary: Smooth matrix recording captures an emotionally charged show highlighted by a soaring Scarlet Begonias solo, heartfelt Black Peter and a rousing Big Railroad Blues.
review: The performance on 12‑28‑86 is remembered for Jerry’s moving Black Peter, the high‑energy opening with Big Railroad Blues and a hot second solo on Scarlet Begonias that even drew an amazed Bob.  The matrix source sounds crisp and well‑balanced – reviewers note a “very smooth soundboard” mix with only minor low‑end roughness on Cold Rain – making it an enjoyable listen despite mixed opinions about the overall set.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality averages around a 3/5 (mixed praise and criticism) while recording quality is consistently rated good‑to‑excellent; weighted combination yields ~3.4.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Smooth matrix mix with clear instrument separation; a slight raunchy low end on Cold Rain but overall crisp and very good sound quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as high‑energy with strong openings; emotional mid‑set moments (Black Peter) and a memorable Scarlet solo, though some listeners found the overall show short and generic.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Back in great form – heartfelt Black Peter and strong guitar tone on Scarlet Begonias; appears a bit reserved but emotionally compelling.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for eye‑contact with Jerry and an amazed reaction to the second solo on Scarlet Begonias.
